Appearance
From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia
Australian blues musician and model

Don West
Born
Don West
OriginSydney, Australia
Genressoul[1]
Occupations
  • Singer
  • songwriter
  • model
Instruments
  • Vocals
  • guitar
Years active2019–present
LabelKobalt Music Group (2025+)
Websitewww.don-west.com
Musical artist

Don West is an Australian soul music musician[1] and model.[2][3]

West released Give Me All Your Love in November 2025, which peaked at number 25 on the ARIA Albums Chart.[4]

Life and career

[edit]

2019–present

[edit]

In 2019, West opened for Lime Cordiale without having released a song at that time. Shortly after, West uploaded the track "Money" onto triple j Unearthed which was followed in 2020 with "Equaliser" which featured guest vocals of Hayley Mary.[5]

In November 2024, West released his debut EP and has appeared at festivals including SXSW Sydney and Byron Bay Bluesfest.[2]

On 7 November 2025, West released his debut album, Give Me All Your Love, which peaked at number 25 on the ARIA Albums Chart.

In December 2025, It was confirmed that West had signed with Kobalt Music Group.[6]

[edit]

  • Format: LP, digital download, streaming

Don West

  • Format: digital, LP

Awards and nominations

[edit]

AIR Awards

[edit]

The Australian Independent Record Awards (commonly known informally as AIR Awards) is an annual awards night to recognise, promote and celebrate the success of Australia's Independent Music sector.

Year Nominee / work Award Result Ref.
2025 Don West Best Independent Soul/R&B Album or EP Nominated [8]

APRA Awards

[edit]

The APRA Awards are held in Australia and New Zealand by the Australasian Performing Right Association to recognise songwriting skills, sales and airplay performance by its members annually.

Year Nominee / work Award Result Ref.
2025 "Small Change"
(Don West, Nathan Hawes) 		Most Performed R&B / Soul Work Nominated [9]
2026 "Julia"
(Don West, Nathan Hawes, William Endicott, Zac Olsen, Jacob Parks) 		Most Performed R&B / Soul Work Nominated [9]

J Awards

[edit]

The J Awards are an annual series of Australian music awards that were established by the Australian Broadcasting Corporation's youth-focused radio station Triple J. They commenced in 2005.

Year Nominee / work Award Result Ref.
2025 Don West Unearthed Artist of the Year Nominated [10]

NSW Music Prize

[edit]

The NSW Music Prize aims to "celebrate, support and incentivise" the NSW's most talented artists, with "the aim of inspiring the next generations of stars". It commenced in 2025.[11]

Year Nominee / work Award Result Ref.
2025 Don West NSW Breakthrough Artist of the Year Nominated [12]

Vanda & Young Global Songwriting Competition

[edit]

The Vanda & Young Global Songwriting Competition is an annual competition that "acknowledges great songwriting whilst supporting and raising money for Nordoff-Robbins" and is coordinated by Albert Music and APRA AMCOS. It commenced in 2009.[13]

Year Nominee / work Award Result Ref.
2025 "Small Change" (Nathan Hawes) Vanda & Young Global Songwriting Competition Finalist [14]
